alert: High temperatures can reduce grain filling in rice — consider shade nets or adjusted planting schedule

Flood risk elevated — prepare drainage channels and consider raised nursery beds
Drought conditions detected — switch to short-duration rice varieties if sowing window remains

Abnormal rainfall during wheat season — watch for lodging and fungal infections
Soil moisture dropping — ensure timely irrigation at crown root initiation and flowering stages
High temperatures may affect boll development and fiber quality — ensure adequate irrigation
Low soil moisture is critical during boll filling — consider drip irrigation
Excessive rainfall can cause boll rot — ensure field drainage
Vegetation stress detected — check for pest infestation (pink bollworm, whitefly)
Extreme heat can affect sucrose accumulation — maintain adequate irrigation
Sugarcane is water-intensive — low soil moisture impacts growth rate and juice quality
Waterlogging can cause red rot — ensure field drainage

Moisture stress during pod filling reduces yield — prioritize irrigation if available
Moisture stress affects pegging and pod development — ensure soil moisture at critical stages
High soil temperatures can affect kernel development — mulching recommended
Heat stress during tasseling can sharply reduce yield — ensure irrigation during pollination
Maize is drought-sensitive at silking stage — prioritize irrigation
Maize cannot tolerate waterlogging — ensure raised bed planting in flood-prone areas
Pigeon pea tolerates moderate drought but needs moisture at flowering — monitor soil conditions
Pigeon pea is highly sensitive to waterlogging — avoid low-lying plots
Even drought-hardy pearl millet needs moisture at panicle emergence — monitor closely
Extreme heat can affect grain development — consider OPV varieties for resilience
Mustard is cold-season — elevated temperatures during flowering reduce oil content
Excess moisture promotes Alternaria blight — monitor and apply fungicide if needed
Extended drought reduces nut yield — basin irrigation recommended for young palms
Heat stress causes button shedding — provide adequate mulching and irrigation
Potato tuber formation is impaired above 30°C — consider early harvest or hill-region sourcing
